sections.description: Define and protect network boundaries for systems that handle or route payment activity.

questions.question: Are network controls in place for systems used to enter card data into a virtual terminal?
questions.help_text: Usually the PC and browser used for VT access.

questions.question: If staff work remotely, is VPN or equivalent used so payment activity does not bypass your security controls?
questions.help_text: Reduces card data exposure on home networks.

questions.question: Are basic host firewall and DNS filtering enabled on VT workstations to block known-bad sites?
questions.help_text: Defense in depth for browser-based attacks.

sections.description: Harden systems and software against unsafe defaults and misconfiguration.

questions.question: Are virtual-terminal workstations hardened (no extra admin rights, current patches, secure browser)?
questions.help_text: Treat the VT workstation as sensitive.

questions.question: Are USB ports or removable media restricted where they could be used to exfiltrate card data?
questions.help_text: Group policy or endpoint tool.

sections.description: Collect, store, and dispose of card data only as your business truly needs.

questions.question: Do you prevent saving full PAN to local drives, email, or chat from the VT session?
questions.help_text: Copy-paste and screenshots are common risks.

questions.question: Are card numbers masked or truncated when displayed after entry where the application allows?
questions.help_text: Reduces shoulder surfing and data leaks.

sections.description: Protect card data in motion across networks and manage keys responsibly.

questions.question: Is the virtual terminal session protected with strong TLS end-to-end?
questions.help_text: Check for valid certificates and HTTPS-only.

questions.question: Do you prohibit entering cards over untrusted public Wi-Fi without VPN?
questions.help_text: Coffee-shop Wi-Fi is high risk.

sections.description: Prevent, detect, and respond to malicious software on in-scope systems.

questions.question: Is anti-malware active on systems used for virtual terminal access?
questions.help_text: Required where AV is not prohibited by PCI guidance for the OS.

questions.question: Is the browser limited to approved extensions and kept updated for VT users?
questions.help_text: Reduces drive-by compromise.

sections.description: Control code and configuration changes and keep components patched.

questions.question: Are changes to VT software, browsers, or plugins reviewed before rollout?
questions.help_text: Keeps payment entry stable and safe.

sections.description: Grant access to systems and data on a need-to-know basis.

questions.question: Is VT access limited to named users who need it for their job?
questions.help_text: Disable accounts when roles change.

questions.question: Do VT sessions lock or time out when idle?
questions.help_text: Reduces unattended access.

sections.description: Verify users and administrators before granting access.

questions.question: Do you require multi-factor authentication for remote access into environments where VT is used?
questions.help_text: Especially for VPN into the office.

questions.question: Are passwords or passphrases strong and not reused across personal sites?
questions.help_text: Consider a password manager for staff.

sections.description: Protect devices and facilities that can reach cardholder environments.

questions.question: Are screens positioned to reduce shoulder surfing and are devices locked when unattended?
questions.help_text: Physical and visual privacy for card entry.

questions.question: When VT hardware is retired, is storage wiped or destroyed before reuse or disposal?
questions.help_text: Follow vendor guidance for the device type.

sections.description: Keep useful audit trails and review them for suspicious activity.

questions.question: Do you retain logs that show who accessed the VT and when, where the platform supports it?
questions.help_text: May combine platform logs with VPN logs.

questions.question: Are security logs reviewed periodically for failed logins or odd access patterns?
questions.help_text: Monthly or automated alerts are common.

sections.description: Find and fix weaknesses in systems and applications in scope.

questions.question: Are OS and browser patches applied promptly on VT workstations?
questions.help_text: Critical for malware resistance.

questions.question: Are critical vulnerabilities from vendor advisories tracked to closure for VT software?
questions.help_text: Ticket or risk register entry.

sections.description: Document expectations, vendors, incidents, and ongoing risk awareness.

questions.question: Do you document who may use the VT and acceptable-use rules for card entry?
questions.help_text: Include prohibition on writing down full PAN.

questions.question: Do VT users receive training on phishing and safe handling of cardholder data?
questions.help_text: Short annual refreshers work.

questions.question: Is your VT provider listed and their PCI responsibilities understood?
questions.help_text: Know who patches the hosted VT.
